title: Ashley Still Joins DoorDash Board of Directors
text: SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--DoorDash, Inc. (NYSE: DASH) announced today that Ashley Still has been appointed to the company's board of directors, effective July 10, 2023. Tony Xu, Co-Founder and CEO of DoorDash, Inc., said, “Ashley has an exceptional track record of leading and scaling businesses of all sizes while tackling complex operational challenges. title: DoorDash: Undervalued Even After A Massive Rally
text: DoorDash has demonstrated staggering revenue growth over the past five years mainly thanks to solid tailwinds related to the COVID-19 pandemic. But the recent quarterly performance suggests that growth momentum is still very strong, and the management is committed to the profitability improvement. Moreover, my valuation analysis suggests the stock is very attractively valued.

title: Judge temporarily blocks NYC's food delivery minimum wage law

title: DoorDash, UberEats, and Grubhub sue New York City over a new $18 an hour minimum wage for delivery drivers
text: Uber, DoorDash, and Grubhub are suing New York City to block a new minimum wage law for delivery workers. Beginning on July 12, delivery apps must pay drivers about $18 per hour they are active on the app.

title: New York's Minimum Wage Law Sends Shockwaves Through Gig Economy
text: Uber and DoorDash face significant operational challenges as New York City's new minimum wage law, tripling base pay for gig workers, threatens to disrupt their cost structures and impact their path to profitability.

title: Uber, DoorDash sue NYC over minimum-wage law
text: The law will require that drivers be paid $17.96 an hour, which will rise to nearly $20 in April 2025. Companies can decide whether to pay drivers hourly or per delivery.

title: Uber, DoorDash sue New York City over minimum wage law
text: Uber Technologies Inc , DoorDash Inc and other app-based food delivery companies filed lawsuits on Thursday seeking to strike down New York City's novel law setting a minimum wage for drivers.

title: DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub Sue New York City Over Wage Law
text: The companies say new minimum pay rates are based on flawed calculus and will raise consumer prices and limit worker flexibility.

title: This Stock Could Get Wrecked by the Student Loan Payment Restart
text: A significant chunk of DoorDash's customer base will have less money to spend later this year when student loan payments restart. The company has yet to become profitable, even during the pandemic, when business was booming.
